title: Listening: News and Event Planning
learningGoal: Understand formal passive voice in a news context, and identify causative verbs in an event planning context.
instructions: Listen to a news report followed by an event manager delegating tasks. Pay close attention to verbs ending in 'gaya/jayega' (passive) and 'waa' (causative delegation).
listeningGoal: Rapidly decode whether an action is being performed by the speaker, being delegated by the speaker, or happening to the subject without a named actor.
audioScenario: A brief news bulletin, followed by an event manager organizing a wedding.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: न्यूज़ एंकर: आज दिल्ली में एक नया पुल खोला गया।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: News Anchor: Aaj Delhi mein ek naya pul khola gaya.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: News Anchor: Today, a new bridge was opened in Delhi.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: न्यूज़ एंकर: कल प्रधानमंत्री के द्वारा भाषण दिया जाएगा।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: News Anchor: Kal Pradhan Mantri ke dwara bhashan diya jayega.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: News Anchor: Tomorrow, a speech will be given by the Prime Minister.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मैनेजर: सुनो, शादी की तैयारियाँ कैसी चल रही हैं?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Manager: Suno, shaadi ki taiyariyan kaisi chal rahi hain?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Manager: Listen, how are the wedding preparations going?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: असिस्टेंट: मैंने हॉल बुक कर लिया है।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Assistant: Maine hall book kar liya hai.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Assistant: I have booked the hall.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मैनेजर: बहुत बढ़िया। क्या तुमने मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया?
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Manager: Bahut badhiya. Kya tumne mehmanon ko khana khilaya?
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Manager: Very good. Did you feed the guests?

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: असिस्टेंट: नहीं, खाना कैटरर से बनवाया जा रहा है।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Assistant: Nahi, khana caterer se banwaya jaa raha hai.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Assistant: No, the food is being gotten made by the caterer.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: मैनेजर: ठीक है। मैं शाम तक सारे कार्ड भिजवा दूँगा।
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Manager: Theek hai. Main shaam tak saare card bhijwa doonga.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Manager: Okay. I will have all the cards sent by evening.

audioTranscriptFull: न्यूज़ एंकर: आज दिल्ली में एक नया पुल खोला गया। कल प्रधानमंत्री के द्वारा भाषण दिया जाएगा। मैनेजर: सुनो, शादी की तैयारियाँ कैसी चल रही हैं? असिस्टेंट: मैंने हॉल बुक कर लिया है। मैनेजर: बहुत बढ़िया। क्या तुमने मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया? असिस्टेंट: नहीं, खाना कैटरर से बनवाया जा रहा है। मैनेजर: ठीक है। मैं शाम तक सारे कार्ड भिजवा दूँगा।

questionSet.questionText: In the news report, 'Ek naya pul khola gaya'. What does this literally mean?
options: A new bridge went opened (Passive Voice: Was opened).

questionSet.questionText: How does the anchor express 'BY the Prime Minister'?
When the manager asks about feeding the guests, what verb does he use?
options: Khilaya (First Causative: Did you feed/make them eat?)
Khilwaya (Second Causative: Did you have someone feed them?)

questionSet.questionText: Listen to the assistant's reply: 'Khana caterer se banwaya jaa raha hai'. What grammar structure is this?
options: A combination of Causative (banwaya) and Passive (jaa raha hai).

questionSet.questionText: What does the manager say he will do with the cards?
options: He will get them sent by someone else (Bhijwa doonga).
He will send them himself (Bhej doonga).

correctAnswers.lq_1: A new bridge went opened (Passive Voice: Was opened).
correctAnswers.lq_3: Khilaya (First Causative: Did you feed/make them eat?)
correctAnswers.lq_4: A combination of Causative (banwaya) and Passive (jaa raha hai).
correctAnswers.lq_5: He will get them sent by someone else (Bhijwa doonga).

feedbackRules.onPass: Brilliant! You can hear the 'waa' infix and instantly know that someone is delegating work.
feedbackRules.onFail: Review the difference between Bhejna (To send) and Bhijwana (To get sent). That 'waa' sound completely changes the meaning.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Sentence 6 ('Banwaya jaa raha hai') is very complex. It means 'It is being gotten made'. It's causative AND passive!

audioAssets.audioTranscript: न्यूज़ एंकर: आज दिल्ली में एक नया पुल खोला गया। कल प्रधानमंत्री के द्वारा भाषण दिया जाएगा। मैनेजर: सुनो, शादी की तैयारियाँ कैसी चल रही हैं? असिस्टेंट: मैंने हॉल बुक कर लिया है। मैनेजर: बहुत बढ़िया। क्या तुमने मेहमानों को खाना खिलाया? असिस्टेंट: नहीं, खाना कैटरर से बनवाया जा रहा है। मैनेजर: ठीक है। मैं शाम तक सारे कार्ड भिजवा दूँगा।

hintText: Listen for 'Ke dwara' in the news broadcast. It is the formal Hindi way of saying 'By [Person]'.
